title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Active Voltage Control on Power Distribution Networks
authors: Jianhong Wang; Wangkun Xu; Yunjie Gu; Wenbin Song; Tim C. Green
problem: active voltage control on distribution networks
key_idea: formulates AVC as a Dec-POMDP and provides an open-source MARL environment
strengths: direct task match and strong baseline relevance
relevance_to_project: primary task-level baseline and problem-context paper

title: Learning Topology Actions for Power Grid Control: A Graph-Based Soft-Label Imitation Learning Approach
authors: Mohamed Hassouna; Clara Holzhüter; Malte Lehna; Matthijs de Jong; Jan Viebahn; Bernhard Sick; Christoph Scholz
problem: grid topology control under dynamic conditions
key_idea: graph-based soft-label imitation learning for topology actions
weaknesses: not federated and not the same AVC setting

title: An Efficient Framework for Clustered Federated Learning
authors: Avishek Ghosh; Jichan Chung; Dong Yin; Kannan Ramchandran
key_idea: cluster clients and train cluster models efficiently
relevance_to_project: method-side foundation for clustered aggregation framing

title: FIELDING: Clustered Federated Learning with Data Drift
authors: Minghao Li; Dmitrii Avdiukhin; Rana Shahout; Nikita Ivkin; Vladimir Braverman; Minlan Yu
key_idea: selective reclustering for heterogeneous clients under drift
relevance_to_project: relevant to heterogeneity and shift narrative

title: FedDiSC: A Computation-efficient Federated Learning Framework for Power Systems Disturbance and Cyber Attack Discrimination
authors: Muhammad Akbar Husnoo; Adnan Anwar; Haftu Tasew Reda; Nasser Hosseinzadeh; Shama Naz Islam; Abdun Naser Mahmood; Robin Doss
problem: privacy-preserving power-systems disturbance and cyberattack discrimination

title: FeDiSa: A Semi-asynchronous Federated Learning Framework for Power System Fault and Cyberattack Discrimination
authors: Muhammad Akbar Husnoo; Adnan Anwar; Haftu Tasew Reda; Nasser Hosseizadeh; Shama Naz Islam; Abdun Naser Mahmood; Robin Doss
problem: power-system fault and cyberattack discrimination with stragglers
